title: Jake Merrick Elected to Oklahoma State Senate!
author: James Silberman
orientation: A short Free the States news post celebrating Jake Merrick's April 2021 special-election victory in Oklahoma SD22, framing it as the second consecutive abolitionist win against well-funded opponents and previewing his pairing with Senator Warren Hamilton to push total and immediate abolition bills.
audiences: Christian voters weighing pro-life vs. abolitionist candidates
Pastors and church leaders mobilizing congregations

questions.question: What was Jake Merrick's margin of victory in the SD22 special general election?
questions.answer: Merrick took more than 65 percent of the vote against Democrat Molly Ooten in the Tuesday special general election to win the SD22 seat.
questions.quote: With more than 65 percent of the vote, Jake Merrick defeated Democrat Molly Ooten in Tuesday’s special general election to become the next Senator from OK SD22.

questions.question: How does Merrick's win compare to Warren Hamilton's 2020 race in terms of money spent?
questions.answer: Both abolitionists won despite being heavily outspent—Hamilton beat incumbent Larry Boggs even though Boggs and his supporting PACs outspent him roughly four-to-one, mirroring Merrick's victory over better-funded Republican and Democrat opponents.
questions.quote: A similar situation played out last year when abolitionist Warren Hamilton defeated incumbent Senator Larry Boggs despite Boggs and Boggs-supporting PACs outspending Hamilton roughly four-to-one.

questions.question: What lesson does Free the States draw from these back-to-back Oklahoma upsets?
questions.audience: Christian voters weighing pro-life vs. abolitionist candidates
questions.answer: Money is being eclipsed by clear, biblically grounded positions—especially on abortion—so candidates with consistently Christian convictions can defeat establishment-backed opponents.
questions.quote: In Oklahoma, it’s becoming clear that money is no substitute for strong, consistently Christian stances on important issues, beginning with abortion.

questions.question: When did Jake Merrick first get on abolitionists' radar?
questions.answer: He drew abolitionist attention during his earlier run for Oklahoma's fifth congressional district when he made abolishing abortion the headline plank of his platform.
questions.quote: Merrick first appeared on the radar of many abolitionists when, during his previous candidacy for Oklahoma’s fifth congressional district, he made “Abolish[ing] abortion” his campaign’s top priority.

questions.question: What specific commitment did Merrick make to voters about abortion legislation?
questions.answer: He pledged to draft legislation ending abortion in Oklahoma, citing the 4,995 abortions performed in the state in 2019 as proof that the status quo must change.
questions.quote:
When elected as State Senator of District 22, I intend to represent and protect the rights of Oklahomans by drafting legislation that will:
>
> 1.) End Abortion in Oklahoma.
>
> In 2019 there were 4,995 abortions performed in Oklahoma. Abortion is currently legal in Oklahoma, and this needs to change. We must end this egregious practice in our state.

questions.question: How does Free the States frame the strategic significance of pairing Merrick with Warren Hamilton?
questions.answer: FTS sees the two senators as future co-authors and floor champions for total and immediate abolition bills, doubling abolitionist firepower in the Oklahoma legislature.
questions.quote: Keep Merrick and Hamilton in your prayers and prepare to support them as they come together as co-authors and champions of bills of total and immediate abolition in the state of Oklahoma.

questions.question: What does the article ask supporters to do beyond celebrating?
questions.audience: Pastors and church leaders mobilizing congregations
questions.answer: It calls readers to pray for both senators and to be ready to actively support them when abolition bills are filed and brought to the floor.
questions.quote: We look forward to supporting Senator-elect Merrick as he joins Senator Hamilton in the Oklahoma Legislature.
